expectations.evidence: spec.md exists at with_skill/outputs/spec.md with 9,319 chars covering all required sections

expectations.text: The transcript shows at least 2 clarifying questions were asked before the spec was written
expectations.evidence: transcript.md shows 8 rounds of clarifying questions covering: assignment triggers, definition of 'involved', reminder timing, email vs in-app per event type, user preferences, read behavior, retention, and existing infrastructure — all before any spec was written

expectations.text: The spec contains a 'What We Are NOT Solving' section with at least one item
expectations.evidence: spec.md has 'What We Are NOT Solving' section with 7 explicit items including user preferences, push notifications, comment emails, repeating reminders, individual dismiss, WebSockets, and mobile push

expectations.text: The spec contains an Interfaces section that names at least one schema or contract
expectations.evidence: spec.md has a detailed Interfaces section with: notifications table schema (all columns with types/constraints), tasks.reminder_sent_at new column, existing tables schema, GET /api/notifications JSON contract, UserMailer methods contract, and two Sidekiq job contracts

expectations.text: The spec does not prescribe specific implementation technologies (e.g. no specific gems, queue systems, or libraries recommended)
expectations.evidence: The spec only references Sidekiq, ActionMailer, and SendGrid because the simulated user named these as existing infrastructure — not as new recommendations. No new implementation technologies are prescribed.
